label: Starter Neutral
description: Quiet near-monochrome Next.js starter. Built as a safe extension-first baseline when the prompt is generic and the future direction is still open.
signatureMotif: quiet near-monochrome shell, restrained tokens, and extension-first clarity
promptHints: Stay intentionally minimal and extension-friendly when the prompt is underspecified — this is a baseline, not a finished product.
Do not pretend to be a polished marketing or product site unless the prompt clearly demands it; one calm primary accent is enough.
Let spacing and typography carry the design instead of decorative chrome, illustrations, or stacked accent colors.

layouts: Use a centered single-column shell with a slim max-width, compact top bar, and evenly spaced content sections divided by quiet borders.
Build heroes as left-aligned headline stacks with one primary action, one secondary link, and an optional small code or preview panel beside them.
Compose feature areas as simple two-by-two card grids with equal heights, muted surfaces, and short copy instead of illustrated storytelling blocks.
Lay out docs or settings pages with a narrow sidebar, sticky section headings, and a calm content pane using consistent row spacing.
Use forms and auth screens as centered cards with minimal helper text, single-column fields, and a restrained footer of secondary links.
motifs: Apply near-monochrome surfaces with one cool primary accent, 1px low-contrast borders, and medium radius cards that feel default but polished.
Keep typography quiet with Geist or Inter throughout, modest size jumps, medium weights, and generous line-height instead of bold display moments.
Use sparse interface chrome: muted section dividers, subtle hover fills, soft focus rings, and almost no decorative background treatment.
Let spacing carry the design, with clean vertical rhythm, predictable gutters, and components that align to a simple baseline grid.
antiPatterns: Avoid full-bleed marketing spectacle like oversized gradient heroes, glowing orbs, and layered parallax scenes that imply a finished brand system.
Never overload starter pages with dense dashboard widgets, data-heavy chrome, or complex multicolumn layouts before the prompt clearly asks for them.
Avoid loud visual personalities such as playful blobs, retro textures, glassmorphism panels, or hand-crafted illustrations that reduce extension flexibility.
Do not stack multiple accent colors, pill badges, or promotional ribbons that make the scaffold feel product-specific on first glance.

themeTokens.bodyBackgroundImage: radial-gradient(circle at top left, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 8%, transparent) 0%, transparent 32%), radial-gradient(circle at bottom right, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-foreground) 4%, transparent) 0%, transparent 38%)
